---
title = "Configuration"
---

*Calepin* keeps configuration minimal. The TOML file handles what needs to be structured data: targets, navigation, and rendering defaults. Everything else (navigation bars, table of contents, font sizes, colors, page layout) is controlled by editing templates and CSS directly. See [Templates](../templates/templates.html) for details.

*Calepin* uses [TOML](https://toml.io) for all configuration. Settings can appear in document front matter (between `---` delimiters) or in `_calepin/config.toml` for collections. Settings are resolved in order, with later values winning: built-in defaults, then `config.toml`, then front matter, then CLI (`-s key=value`).

# TOML basics

Strings, numbers, and booleans:

```toml
title = "My Document"
dpi = 150.0
number-sections = true
```

Tables group related settings under a header:

```toml
[highlight]
light = "github"
dark = "nord"
```

Arrays of tables use double brackets. Each `[[contents]]` block adds one section to the sidebar:

```toml
[[contents]]
title = "Guide"
pages = ["intro.qmd", "advanced.qmd"]

[[contents]]
title = "Reference"
pages = ["api.qmd"]
```

Inline tables work for simple cases:

```toml
highlight = {light = "github", dark = "nord"}
```

Lists use square brackets:

```toml
bibliography = ["refs.bib", "extra.bib"]
pages = ["chapter1.qmd", "chapter2.qmd", "appendix/*.qmd"]
```

# Config resolution

Settings are resolved in order, with later values overriding earlier ones:

1. Built-in defaults
   - `src/config/shared.toml`: rendering, figures, code execution, labels.
   - `src/config/document.toml`: single-document targets (html, latex, typst, pdf, etc.).
   - `src/config/collection.toml`: collection targets (website, book).
2. Project config
   - `_calepin/config.toml`: project-level settings for collections.
3. Sidecar config
   - `{stem}_calepin/config.toml`: per-document overrides.
4. Document front matter
   - TOML between `---` delimiters at the top of the `.qmd` file.
5. CLI overrides
   - `-s key=value` flags passed to `calepin render`.

# Shared defaults

These apply to both single documents and collections:

```toml
csl = "chicago-author-date"
math = "katex"
lang = "en"
embed_resources = true
dpi = 150.0

[highlight]
light = "github"
dark = "nord"

[figure]
fig_width = 6.0
fig_asp = 0.618
out_width = 0.70
device = "png"
alignment = "center"

[execute]
cache = true
eval = true
echo = true
include = true
warning = true
message = true
error = true
comment = "> "
results = "markup"

[toc]
enabled = false
depth = 3
title = "Contents"

[labels]
abstract_title = "Abstract"
keywords = "Keywords"
appendix = "Appendix"
citation = "Citation"
reuse = "Reuse"
funding = "Funding"
copyright = "Copyright"
listing = "Listing"
proof = "Proof"
contents = "Contents"
```

# Document targets

Built-in targets for single documents:

```toml
[targets.html]
writer = "html"
template = "page"
extension = "html"
fig_extension = "svg"
preview = "serve"

[targets.latex]
writer = "latex"
template = "page"
extension = "tex"
fig_extension = "pdf"
preview = "serve"

[targets.typst]
writer = "typst"
template = "page"
extension = "typ"
fig_extension = "svg"
preview = "serve"

[targets.pdf]
writer = "typst"
extension = "pdf"
fig_extension = "svg"
compile = "typst compile {input}"

[targets.revealjs]
writer = "html"
extension = "html"
fig_extension = "svg"
preview = "serve"

[targets.markdown]
writer = "markdown"
extension = "md"
fig_extension = "png"
preview = "none"
```

# Collection defaults

Additional defaults for multi-document builds (websites, books):

```toml
global_crossref = false
preview_port = 3456

[targets.website]
writer = "html"
template = "page"
extension = "html"
fig_extension = "svg"
embed_resources = false
preview = "serve"

[targets.book]
writer = "typst"
template = "page"
extension = "pdf"
fig_extension = "svg"
compile = "typst compile {input}"
```

# Collection config example

A typical `_calepin/config.toml`:

```toml
title = "My Site"
target = "website"
output = "output"

[[contents]]
pages = ["index.qmd", "about.qmd"]

[[contents]]
title = "Guide"
pages = ["guide/*.qmd"]

[[navbar.left]]
text = "My Site"
href = "/index.html"

[[navbar.right]]
icon = "github"
href = "https://github.com/user/repo"

[[post]]
command = "pagefind --site {output}"

[var]
sidebar_collapse_level = 0
```
